disease.name: Gram Stain Interpretation Guide
disease.note: Gram stain is the first critical step in microbiologic diagnosis. Classifies bacteria as Gram-positive (purple) or Gram-negative (pink/red) based on cell wall peptidoglycan. Morphology (cocci, bacilli, coccobacilli, spirilla) and arrangement (clusters, chains, pairs) guide empiric therapy.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If organisms seen in sterile site + PMNs -> INFECTION; if no organisms -> consider culture result

subtypes.name: Gram-Positive Cocci in Clusters (Staphylococci)
subtypes.management: Presumptive Staph aureus (if coagulase+) or Staph epidermidis (if coagulase-). Start empiric vancomycin if MRSA risk. Nafcillin/cefazolin if MSSA. Follow-up with culture and sensitivity.

subtypes.name: Gram-Positive Cocci in Chains (Streptococci)
subtypes.management: Empiric ceftriaxone or penicillin. Group A strep: penicillin + clindamycin. Group B: ampicillin. Strep pneumo: ceftriaxone + vancomycin until sensitivity (PCN/cef resistance common).

subtypes.name: Gram-Negative Bacilli (Enteric/Rods)
subtypes.management: Empiric broad coverage (cefepime, piperacillin-tazobactam, or carbapenem if ESBL risk). Tailor per culture and sensitivity. Consider local antibiogram resistance patterns.

subtypes.name: Gram-Negative Cocci (Neisseria, Moraxella)
subtypes.management: N. meningitidis: IV ceftriaxone + dexamethasone. N. gonorrhoeae: ceftriaxone 500mg IM + azithromycin 1g PO. Respiratory isolation for meningitidis until 24h of antibiotics.

subtypes.name: Gram-Positive Bacilli (Listeria, Bacillus, Clostridium)
subtypes.management: Listeria: ampicillin + gentamicin. Bacillus anthracis: ciprofloxacin + clindamycin. Clostridium: metronidazole. Anthrax requires public health notification.

laboratory.name: Culture and sensitivity on solid and liquid media
Coagulase test
laboratory.indication: differentiate S. aureus (positive) from other Staph

management.protocolReference: CLSI Guidelines for Gram Stain, IDSA Treatment Guidelines

pathways.criteria: organisms seen on Gram stain + PMNs from sterile site
pathways.treatment: Begin empiric antibiotics based on Gram stain pattern. Adjust when culture and sensitivity available. Narrow spectrum when possible.

pathways.criteria: no bacteria on Gram stain, PMNs present or absent
pathways.treatment: Consider culture results (may still be positive). Start empiric therapy if clinical suspicion high. Consider atypical organisms (AFB, fungus) if negative repeatedly.
